status: canonical schema candidate; not a mass-spectrum proof
goal: Define the vector state needed to derive fermion masses from Euler-Berry holonomy without treating effective Yukawa couplings as primitive inputs.

state_vector.V_f: direct_sum(r_f, h_f, c_s, g_f, z_s, validation_target_optional)
state_vector.r_f: representation vector: color dimension, weak dimension, chirality, hypercharge Y, T3, electric charge Q
state_vector.h_f: holonomy vector: Phi_AB, Phi_Berry, Phi_Euler, Theta_I, D_Lambda, epsilon_nu, integer closure N_f
state_vector.c_s: Collatz orbit vector: twin-prime seed, orbit length, parity word, sign-flip count, normalized Collatz cost
state_vector.g_f: geometry vector: Fubini-Study distance, Berry enclosed area, Euler spin closure, Poincare disk geodesic depth
state_vector.z_s: spectral vector: zeta-polar labels and tetrahedral-depth labels when explicitly mapped
state_vector.validation_target_optional: observed masses or required actions may be stored only as validation targets, never as derivation inputs

canonical_mass_form.effective_yukawa_shadow: y_eff(f)=exp(-S_EB(f)/kappa) times phase/unitary factors, after low-energy projection
canonical_mass_form.mass: m_f = v/sqrt(2) * |y_eff(f)| for charged fermions after electroweak orientation selection
canonical_mass_form.operator: M_hol(f)=M0 Pi_R P exp(i integral_gamma_f A_EBI) Pi_L exp(-S_EB(f)/kappa)
canonical_mass_form.warning: pure holonomy has unit modulus; hierarchy requires a real positive Euler-Berry action S_EB, not just a phase

open_noncanonical_blocks: explicit map from zeta-polar labels to tetrahedral-depth coordinates
explicit Poincare disk embedding of Collatz orbits
derivation of S_EB(f) from V_f without using observed masses
validation_gates: no PDG or observed mass may enter S_EB derivation
observed masses may only appear in validation_target_optional
anomaly cancellation must remain exact on rational charges
effective Yukawa couplings are low-energy names, not primitive parameters
a successful mass-sector claim requires predicted S_EB(f), then comparison to validation targets
